summary: emit never reaps ownerless projections — a removed member's file stays on disk still loading; reap iff byte-identical to its lock fingerprint, report the drifted orphan, never silent-delete

edit.description: `emit` (:436) writes the current members' projections and rewrites the lock whole, but never reads the prior lock — a member dropped from the program leaves its projection on disk with the lock forgetting the path. Before the rewrite, read the prior `lock.toml`'s provenance rows (source_path + emit_hash, the same lock read `config_stale`:806 / `emit_owned_targets`:874 already do), diff against the current `payload.members` projected source_paths, and for each lock-known projection with no current owner: delete it iff the on-disk bytes hash to its recorded `emit_hash` (reap — temper wrote every byte, nothing authored is lost), else leave the file and surface a drift finding (never a silent delete). Extend `EmitReport`/`EmitEntry`/`EmitOutcome` (:436 region) with reaped and orphan-drift outcomes and render them in `render_emit`:636 so 'N unchanged' is no longer the whole story when an owner disappeared. Rider (comment staleness, per the residue-sweep exception): the `Payload`/`declarations` docstrings at :262/:269 still read 'five families'/'five declaration families' — the mention edge shipped `mentions` as the sixth declaration family; cut the stale count on contact (rust.md exit-clause).

tests.asserts: re-emitting after a member is removed deletes its projection when the on-disk file is byte-identical to its lock emit fingerprint (reap); a removed member whose projection was hand-edited is left on disk and reported as an orphan-drift finding (never silent-delete); the emit report distinguishes reaped from drifted-orphan

acceptance: Re-emitting after a member is dropped from the program reaps its projection when the on-disk file matches the lock's emit fingerprint, and reports a finding while leaving the file when it has drifted — never a silent delete; the emit report distinguishes reaped from drifted-orphan.
notes: Field report 2026-07-07 -> decision 0007. Detection is decidable: a lock-known projection with no current owning member. The hash is the safety line (verbatim invariant's spirit — never delete hand-edited bytes). Prior-lock read mirrors config_stale:806 / emit_owned_targets:874. Now carries the drift.rs 'five families' comment rider (:262/:269 — mention edge is the 6th declaration family; cut on contact). f4189c3 fully routed. scoped at 2efd00b.

summary: emit writes projection bytes verbatim including a source's CRLF; normalize every emitted projection to LF uniformly so double-emit byte-reproduces across platforms (a CRLF source emits clean)

edit.description: `emit_one`:516 assembles `desired` (and the `second_pass` determinism check) from either `project_bytes`:607 or, for an own_path member, `projection.body` verbatim — no line-ending normalization, so a member body carrying CRLF (a body authored on Windows) lands CRLF in the projection and the same program emits different bytes on different machines. Normalize `desired`/`second_pass` to LF (CRLF and lone CR → LF) before the double-emit compare, before `sha256_hex`:573, and before `fs::write`:585, so the lock's emit_hash and the written bytes are the LF-normalized form. Both branches normalize — the own_path body (a file()-carried member's own source) too, per pipeline.md's 'projections are written LF uniformly, whatever the source's convention.'

tests.asserts: a member whose body carries CRLF (and one carrying a lone CR) emits an LF-only projection on disk; the emit_hash and the idempotent Unchanged re-emit both compute over the LF-normalized bytes, so double-emit byte-reproduces regardless of the source's line-ending convention

acceptance: A member body carrying CRLF (or lone CR) emits an LF-only projection; the emit hash and written bytes are the normalized form, so double-emit byte-reproduces on any platform — line endings never reach a projection as content.
notes: Field ruling 0010 (spec delta f4189c3): EOL is layout, not content — the verbatim invariant guards words, one byte wider than leaf whitespace. Emit-only; the import/read side is unchanged. own_path body (a file()-carried member's own source) also normalizes per 'uniformly, whatever the source's convention.' Shares src/drift.rs emit + tests/emit.rs with EMIT-REAP-ORPHANS → blockedBy it. 0008/0009/0011 unrouted. scoped at d69987f.

summary: coverage note scans only its curated known-surface list, so a stray ungoverned `.claude/` entry sails through silent — flag any `.claude/` entry no kind or known surface covers as a warn advisory

per.section: The named expansion — what the model reaches next

edit.description: `check`:103 iterates only the curated `KNOWN_SURFACES`:64 list, so an entry under `.claude/` that is on no built-in/locked kind's locus AND on no known surface (a bogus `.clauignore`, a stray `.claude/notes.md`) is examined by nothing and passes silently. Add a scan of the actual entries directly under `<root>/.claude/`: for each, skip it when a governing kind covers it (the `governs`:202 / `governed_by_any`:192 logic, generalized off `KnownSurface` to an arbitrary relative path, reading the same built-in + locked-kind set `with_locked_kinds`:159 assembles) or a `KNOWN_SURFACES` entry already names it (those keep their richer `UNMODELED_RULE`:28 `coverage.unmodeled-surface` message — never double-report), else emit a new warn `coverage.unclaimed-entry` diagnostic naming the stray path. Honor the repository's ignore rules (a gitignored `.claude/` entry is by declaration not authored here — the `ignore` crate, as discovery does), so a gitignored stray never fires. Warn severity only — never gates, session-start ignores it, exactly as the existing coverage findings. Contained here: `check(root, kinds, member_counts)` already carries `root`; no main.rs:744 call-site change.

tests.asserts: a stray `.claude/` entry no kind governs and no known surface names (a `.clauignore`, a stray file) fires exactly one warn `coverage.unclaimed-entry` finding; a governed locus (`.claude/skills`) and a curated known surface (`.claude/agents`, `.claude/settings.json`) never fire it — no double-report with `coverage.unmodeled-surface`; a gitignored stray fires nothing; the finding is warn severity (never error, never a session-start verdict)

acceptance: A `.claude/` entry that no in-scope kind governs and no curated known surface names — a bogus `.clauignore` — is surfaced as a warn `coverage.unclaimed-entry` advisory rather than sailing through silent; governed loci and known surfaces do not double-report, a gitignored stray never fires, and the finding never gates.
notes: Field report 2026-07-07 -> decision 0011: vocabulary now covers documented capability, so what remains unclaimed under `.claude/` is a genuine stray, not a vocab gap — the advisory becomes shippable. Distinct rule from `coverage.unmodeled-surface`. Contained to src/coverage_note.rs + in-module tests via the existing check(root,kinds,member_counts) inputs; collision-free. 0011's command/agent kinds + user-invoked value stay derivable-later; f4189c3 not fully routed. scoped at 34dee6e.

summary: the runtime guard regex-matches any .claude/ file_path without the lock; for a file()-carried member that .claude/ file IS its authored source, so block denies editing the source of truth

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

edit.description: `guard`:594 delegates to `targets_projection`:606, which tests the payload's `file_path` against the `.claude/` regex `GUARD_PATH_MATCH`:109 alone — no lock. So under `block` a write to a file()-carried member's own `.claude/` source (its authored source of truth, which `drift::emit_owned_targets`:874 already excludes from the projection set via `if own_path continue`) is wrongly denied. Widen `guard` to take the lock's emit-owned projection paths (from `drift::emit_owned_targets`, the pub reader install's own placements already bind to) and bind on set membership: a `file_path` naming a lock-declared projection stays bound (block/warn/note per mode), any other `.claude/` write — an own_path source or unmanaged file — returns `GuardVerdict::Allow`:594. Path compare rides the lock's now-`/`-normalized `source_path` (PATH-SEP-NORMALIZE shipped). Retire GUARD_PATH_MATCH or keep it as a cheap pre-filter — build's call.

The `Command::Guard` handler (:326-341) derives `mode` via `mode_from_lock`:498 but calls `install::guard(&payload, mode)`:341 with no projection set — the runtime guard stays regex-bound while install's placements are already lock-grounded. Read the lock's emit-owned projections (`drift::emit_owned_targets(&path.join(TEMPER_DIR))`, the same lock `mode_from_lock` already consults) and thread them into the widened `install::guard` call, so the per-call guard verdict is lock-grounded too.

The existing guard tests drive the CLI against a lock via `run_guard`:632 (`guard_reads_the_block_posture...`:656, `guard_defaults_to_warn...`:679), so the widened `install::guard` signature is internal and they keep passing. Add a case that lifts a file()-carried member whose own_path `.claude/` source is written.

tests.asserts: driving via the CLI (run_guard), a write to a file()-carried member's own .claude/ source (own_path — absent from the lock's emit-owned projection set) passes (Allow) under block posture, while a write to a lock-declared projected .claude/ file still binds at the declared mode; the no-lock default-warn and block-posture cases still hold

acceptance: The runtime guard passes a write to a file()-carried member's own .claude/ source (own_path) and continues to bind writes to lock-declared projected .claude/ files, reading the lock's projection set (drift::emit_owned_targets) rather than a path regex alone.
notes: Field report 1. PATH-SEP-NORMALIZE blocker shipped 402e5cc/2efd00b — this ship audit clears the gate. Mechanism: bind the runtime guard to the existing pub `drift::emit_owned_targets`:874, which already excludes own_path rows — no drift.rs/kind.rs edit, disjoint from the EMIT-* drift.rs chain; own_path .claude/ sources fall through to Allow. SETTINGS-FORMAT-PRESERVING blockedBy it. scoped at 75798a5.

summary: install's settings write re-serializes the whole settings.json (keys reordered, EOL churn) — make the hook merge format-preserving so existing keys/order/formatting survive the insertion

edit.description: `project_settings`:665 parses the existing `.claude/settings.json`, grafts the `SessionStart`/`PreToolUse` temper hooks, then re-emits the whole document via `serde_json::to_string_pretty`:733 — so a human's key order, indentation, and EOL are all rewritten, exactly the churn the round-trip discipline forbids for TOML/markdown. Make the write format-preserving: only the temper hook groups it inserts/updates change bytes; every existing key, its order, and the file's formatting survive (a hand-authored settings.json diffs by one hunk after install). Absent/empty file still writes a fresh canonical document. Mechanism is build's decidable call — a JSON CST-editor crate or a surgical splice of just the two hook arrays; lean surgical (dependency-free, subtraction-before-addition). Update the stale comment at :660-664 which currently asserts the gap ('there is no format-preserving JSON editor to round-trip through') — decision 0008 closes it (exit clause: cut on contact). If a crate is adopted, that is an ordinary Cargo.toml dependency add.

`EXISTING_SETTINGS`:108 is already canonical 2-space pretty JSON, so a whole-file re-serialize is byte-identical to it — the fixture cannot falsify reordering/reformatting. Add a deliberately non-canonical settings fixture (a distinct indentation width and a key order serde would not reproduce) and assert that after install every pre-existing byte outside the grafted temper hook groups is unchanged (one-hunk diff), while the SessionStart/guard placements still land and re-running converges.

tests.asserts: installing into a settings.json with non-canonical formatting (custom indentation, non-serde key order) leaves every existing key's order and formatting byte-intact and diffs by a single hunk — the grafted temper hooks — never re-serializing the whole file; the SessionStart hook and PreToolUse guard still place and install stays idempotent

acceptance: Install merges the SessionStart hook (and the guard, with its constituency) into an existing .claude/settings.json without re-serializing it — existing keys, order, and formatting survive, a hand-authored file diffs by one hunk — while an absent file still writes a fresh canonical document and re-running converges.
notes: Field ruling 0008 (f4189c3): the JSON peer of the toml_edit round-trip keystone. install.rs:660-664 asserts the gap ('no format-preserving JSON editor') — 0008 closes it; update the comment (exit clause). Mechanism — a JSON CST crate vs. a surgical splice of the two temper hook groups — is build's call; lean surgical, dependency-free. Shares install.rs+tests/install.rs with GUARD-OWNPATH -> blockedBy it. 0009/0011 unrouted. scoped at 52d0316.

summary: a file() path resolves against one cwd/baseDir, not the module that states it — scaffold works only by directory-depth accident; recut resolution to the stating module, move scaffold + docs with it

edit.description: `fileSourcePath`:147 resolves a `file()` asset against `options.baseDir ?? process.cwd()` — one workspace/cwd base for every member, never the module that stated the path. Recut resolution so a `file()` path resolves relative to the module that declared it (the rule every import system teaches). The now-vestigial cwd/`baseDir` default and its carriers (`ResolveOptions.baseDir`:23-25, `EmitOptions.baseDir`:185-188, the `baseDir` thread through `emit`:222/`resolveBody`:40) retire or repurpose as the new mechanism needs — build's call. The member's `source_path`:164 must still end absolute (the engine's true source path). Update the module-relative doc comments (:22, :185) which currently describe a cwd base.

`file(path)`:87 / the `File` interface:38 capture only a path string, so resolution cannot be module-relative. Give the constructor the stating module's location — ESM's only facility is `import.meta.url`, so the call carries it (`file(import.meta.url, relPath)` or `file(new URL(relPath, import.meta.url))` — exact spelling is build's decidable call among equivalents). `File.path`:41 (surfaced in emit's error text) and the `Module-relative path`:40 doc move with it.

`relative_to_workspace`:957 writes the scaffolded `file()` path with one `../` (relative to `.temper/`), but member modules live at `.temper/<kind>/<name>.ts` (:885-886) — two levels under root — so under module-relative resolution the path must recut to the member module's own directory (`../../.claude/...`), and `member_module_source`:980 must emit the new `file(...)` call shape (the `file({rel_path:?})` template at :991). Update the stale doc comment :950-953 ('SDK resolves against cwd, which emit_program sets to harness.ts's directory') — the exit clause covers cutting it. The unit tests at :1349/:1367 assert the old `file("../.claude/...")` shape and move with the change.

tests.asserts: a file() asset resolves relative to the module that stated it (not cwd/baseDir): a member whose file() names a path relative to its own module resolves and reads byte-faithfully regardless of the process cwd, its payload source_path is the absolute stating-module-relative path, and a missing asset is still a loud emit error

a scaffolded member module at .temper/<kind>/<name>.ts writes a file() path that resolves module-relative to the source artifact (climbing from the module's own directory, not by cwd accident), and the emitted program's first emit reads that source back byte-identical

acceptance: A file() asset path resolves relative to the module that stated it, never the workspace/cwd; scaffold writes module-relative paths that resolve from the member module's own directory (no longer saved by directory-depth accident); the SDK doc becomes true and emit stays byte-reproducible.
notes: Field ruling 0009 (f4189c3): resolution, scaffold, docs 'move together' — split leaves scaffold emitting one shape while resolution expects another, so one commit. ESM forces import.meta.url as the stating-module facility; call spelling is build's. Scope is file() assets (emit's one path-resolved surface). Shares install.rs+tests/install.rs with the install chain -> blockedBy SETTINGS-FORMAT-PRESERVING. 0011 unrouted. scoped at 4bfa3cd.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.4, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
